title: paeonia × suffruticosa subsp. atava
intro: Paeonia × suffruticosa subsp. atava, also known as the tree peony, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the family Paeoniaceae and is native to China. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides and other compounds. The medicinal properties of Paeonia × suffruticosa subsp. atava make it beneficial for various health issues, including pain relief, fever reduction, and improved blood circulation, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, insomnia, and other conditions. Paeonia × suffruticosa subsp. atava is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed as a fresh plant or dried root.
